skill.name: youtube-automation
skill.description: Use Rube MCP and Composio YouTube tools to upload videos, update metadata, manage playlists, search content, review analytics, and handle comments.
skill.summary: Automate YouTube channel workflows through Rube MCP with schema-aware tool selection.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static external-command and weak-crypto findings are false positives caused by Markdown inline code, code fences, and YouTube field names. The skill is not blocked, but it should carry a medium warning because it directs OAuth-backed YouTube uploads, metadata changes, playlist edits, and subscriptions through an external MCP endpoint.

medium_findings.title: OAuth-Backed YouTube Account Actions
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to connect Rube MCP, complete Google OAuth, and run YouTube tools that can upload videos, update metadata, create playlists, and change subscriptions. This is legitimate for the stated purpose, but mistakes or compromised prompts could affect a real YouTube account.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines directly describe account authorization and state-changing YouTube operations. The intent is functional rather than malicious, so the concern is operational risk rather than confirmed abuse.

low_findings.title: False Positive External Command Findings
low_findings.description: The static analyzer treated Markdown backticks around tool names, parameters, and workflow examples as Ruby shell execution. The file is documentation-only and contains no executable Ruby or shell command blocks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown inline code and numbered workflow text. No evidence found of executable shell invocation or user-controlled command construction.

low_findings.title: Expected External MCP Endpoint
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL points to the documented Rube MCP endpoint used for setup. This creates a network dependency and third-party trust requirement, but it is aligned with the skill purpose.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears in setup instructions and is not paired with hidden data transfer logic. Risk remains because users must trust the external MCP service for YouTube access.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Findings
low_findings.description: The high-risk weak-crypto and reconnaissance hits are triggered by ordinary YouTube documentation terms such as description, update, bytes, item identifiers, and quota monitoring. No evidence found of cryptographic code, hashing, scanning, or host reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the cited lines shows YouTube workflow documentation, not security-sensitive primitives. The analyzer likely matched substrings without semantic context.

content.value_statement: YouTube channel work often requires repeated uploads, playlist edits, searches, and analytics checks. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Rube MCP YouTube tools with current schema lookup.
actual_capabilities: Guides YouTube video upload workflows and metadata updates.
Explains playlist creation, listing, and adding videos to playlists.
Supports YouTube search plus video and channel detail lookup.
Documents channel statistics, activities, and batch video analytics workflows.
Covers subscriptions and listing comment threads on videos.
Highlights quota costs, pagination, ID formats, and response parsing pitfalls.
limitations: Requires a connected Rube MCP server and an active YouTube OAuth connection.
Does not provide its own YouTube API implementation or credentials.
Tool schemas may change, so agents must search tools before each workflow.
State-changing actions can affect a real YouTube channel and should be confirmed.

use_cases.title: Schedule Upload Preparation
use_cases.description: Prepare a video upload with title, description, tags, category, privacy status, and thumbnail steps.

use_cases.title: Organize Channel Playlists
use_cases.description: List playlists, create a new playlist, and add selected videos while handling pagination and ID formats.

use_cases.title: Review Channel Performance
use_cases.description: Resolve channel IDs, collect channel statistics, list videos, and batch video details for performance review.

prompt_templates.title: Check YouTube Connection
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the YouTube automation skill to verify that Rube MCP is available and that my YouTube connection is active. Do not modify my channel.

prompt_templates.title: Upload a Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the YouTube automation skill to upload my prepared video as unlisted. Confirm the title, description, tags, category, and video file object before upload.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Playlist
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the YouTube automation skill to create a playlist for this campaign and add these video IDs. Check existing playlists first to avoid duplicates.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Channel Videos
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the YouTube automation skill to resolve this channel handle, list recent videos, batch fetch video statistics, and summarize performance patterns.

output_examples.input: Prepare an unlisted upload for a product demo video.
output_examples.output: The assistant verifies the active YouTube connection, confirms metadata, checks the current upload schema, uploads the video, and reports the resulting video status.

output_examples.input: Find all videos in a channel uploads playlist and summarize engagement.
output_examples.output: The assistant resolves the channel ID, converts it to the uploads playlist ID, paginates through videos, fetches details in batches, and summarizes views and engagement.

output_examples.input: Create a playlist and add five selected videos.
output_examples.output: The assistant lists existing playlists, confirms the target playlist name, creates it if needed, adds each video, and flags any failed additions.

best_practices: Search Rube MCP tools before every workflow to use current schemas.
Confirm all public uploads, metadata changes, playlist edits, and subscription changes before execution.
Prefer list endpoints over search when possible to reduce YouTube quota use.
anti_patterns: Do not pass a raw local file path where the upload tool expects a video file object.
Do not use a channel ID as a playlist ID without converting uploads playlists correctly.
Do not ignore pagination when listing playlists, comments, activities, or channel videos.

faq.question: Does this skill upload videos directly?
faq.answer: It guides agents to use Rube MCP YouTube tools. Uploads require an active YouTube OAuth connection.

faq.question: Does it need a YouTube API key?
faq.answer: The skill says no API key is needed for setup. It uses the Rube MCP endpoint and Google OAuth connection.

faq.question: Can it manage playlists?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents listing playlists, creating playlists, adding videos, and listing playlist items.

faq.question: Can it fetch analytics?
faq.answer: It can guide channel statistics lookup, channel video listing, and batch video detail collection through YouTube tools.

faq.question: What should users confirm before running it?
faq.answer: Users should confirm account connection, upload metadata, privacy status, playlist edits, and subscription changes.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: The skill has no confirmed malicious code, but it can trigger real YouTube account changes through an external MCP service.
